This release introduces our rename from Repopack to Repoxide, along with automated migration functionality to ensure a smooth transition for all users.

## Important: Project Renamed to Repoxide

Due to legal considerations, this project has been renamed from "Repopack" to "Repoxide". We are committed to ensuring a smooth transition for all users.

### What's New

#### Automated Migration Support (v0.2.1)
- Added functionality to automatically detect and migrate existing Repopack configurations
- Handles migration of:
  - Configuration files (`repopack.config.json` → `repoxide.config.json`)
  - Ignore files (`.repopackignore` → `.repoxideignore`)
  - Instruction files (`repopack-instruction.md` → `repoxide-instruction.md`)
  - Both local and global configurations
- Preserves all user settings during migration

#### Project Rename Changes (v0.2.0)
- Updated all project files and references to use the new name
- Updated npm package name to `repoxide`
- Updated documentation to reflect the new name
- Added support for legacy output file detection

### How to Migrate

We strongly recommend migrating to the new package. Install Repoxide using either:

```bash
npx repoxide
```

or

```bash
npm install -g repoxide
```

The tool will automatically detect your existing Repopack configuration files and offer to migrate them to the new format.

#### Optional: Uninstall Repopack
After confirming Repoxide works for your needs, you can optionally uninstall Repopack:

```bash
npm uninstall -g repopack
```

---

Thank you for being part of our community. We look forward to continuing to serve you as Repoxide! If you have any questions about the migration, please feel free to create an issue on our GitHub repository.
